behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic’s algorithmic quality check itself (route-length ratio), which can be exercised with in-memory coordinate/distance fixtures and precomputed optimal baselines. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and no AI/ML output is involved. Higher levels rejected: Integration/System are disqualified by behavior text focusing on heuristic output quality, not module handoff or infrastructure interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function directly on a corpus of deterministic stop sets (including edge layouts and random seeded sets up to 20 stops) with known optimal lengths; assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This failure commonly appears at local module boundaries: distance output + road-type speed config + estimator assembly. An isolated math-function unit test can miss mapping/wiring defects (e.g., wrong road-type key lookup). Integration brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no real AI/ML dependency; no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real distance-to-segment model, road-type speed configuration loader, and time-window estimator in-process; feed a multi-segment route containing mixed road types and assert per-stop ETA/time-window values match expected calculations from segment distance/speed.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is a pure validation rule on numeric inputs (total estimate vs remaining shift) plus deterministic excess computation. No module interaction is required to expose the core failure mode. Higher levels rejected: Integration/System are disqualified by behavior text centering on validator decision/output, not cross-module/infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with table-driven cases around boundary conditions (equal, just under, just over, far over shift hours) and assert reject/accept plus exact excess duration returned.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure mode is orchestration across modules: geocoding result handling, skip-list reporting, and optimizer input construction. A single-function unit test would miss handoff bugs. Integration brake: no requirement for real network/HTTP semantics in behavior text beyond deterministic 'returns no results', so System is not mandatory; no AI/ML or agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full service pipeline in-process with a stubbed geocoding client returning coordinates for N-1 addresses and empty result for one; assert skipped-address report contains that address and resulting optimized route excludes it while still covering all successfully geocoded stops.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when deduplication output is handed to route-building/optimization and item aggregation; unit tests on dedupe alone can miss duplicate visitation due to downstream wiring. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency; no AI/ML dependency; no multi-step agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the address-ingest + consolidation + route-construction modules together with duplicate-address fixtures carrying distinct item payloads; assert one stop exists for that address, items are merged correctly, and final route contains the address exactly once.
